extension.valueMarkdown: In Arizona she had primary care and cardiology follow-up that established the chronic disease baseline: diabetes requiring intensification, CKD emerging, atrial fibrillation managed with anticoagulation, and HFrEF treated medically. After moving to New Mexico she had an acute decompensated heart-failure hospitalization, then longitudinal follow-up with a heart-failure clinic, nephrology, and retina specialists for diabetic retinopathy with macular edema.

result.display: Fundus photography / dilated fundus exam findings

resource.conclusion: Fundus photography and dilated examination showed scattered microaneurysms, dot-blot hemorrhages, and hard exudates, with greater diabetic retinopathy severity in the left eye and no retinal tear or detachment.

Right eye central macular thickness 289 microns without center-involving edema. Left eye center-involving diabetic macular edema with central subfield thickness 412 microns, supporting same-day intravitreal treatment.

reasonReference.display: Center-involving diabetic macular edema, left eye

dosageInstruction.text: Inject 1.25 mg/0.05 mL intravitreally into the left eye once on 2025-03-06.
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Post-injection precautions reviewed after left-eye intravitreal bevacizumab.

note.text: Single completed intravitreal dose administered during the encounter.

reasonReference.display: Center-involving diabetic macular edema, left eye

followUp.text: Retina follow-up in 4 to 6 weeks for repeat OCT and consideration of additional anti-VEGF injection depending on response.

note.text: Topical anesthesia and povidone-iodine prep were used. Bevacizumab 1.25 mg/0.05 mL was injected intravitreally into the left eye, and post-injection precautions were reviewed.
